sourceMovie.title: The Little Death
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Little Death for the same reason it broke through in 2014: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Josh Lawson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Shortbus
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Little Death clicked for you, Shortbus (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Little Death?
faq.answer: Try Shortbus, Hello, My Name Is Doris, My Awkward Sexual Adventure—each shares DNA with The Little Death while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Little Death?
faq.answer: The Little Death (2014) is best described as Drama, Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Little Death worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
